fixes.code:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    if n % 2 == 1:
        # odd length: use n//2 'a's + 'b' + n//2 'a's
        half = n // 2
        s = 'a' * half + 'b' + 'a' * half
    else:
        # even length: use (n//2 - 1) 'a's + 'b' + 'a' + (n//2 - 1) 'a's + ... 
        # Actually, let's think differently:
        # For even n: use 'ab' repeated? No that won't work.
        # Simple approach: for even n, use (n-1) unique chars approach or
        # n//2 'a's + 'b' + (n//2 - 1) 'a's won't be symmetric
        # 
        # Better: if n is even, we can do (n//2) 'a' + (n//2) 'b'
        # "aaa...abbb...b" - check: 'a' occurs n//2 times (odd if n//2 is odd)
        # This doesn't always work.
        #
        # Reliable approach: 
        # If n is even: output 'a'*(n//2 - 1) + 'b' + 'a' + 'b' + 'a'*(n//2 - 1)
        # Hmm, let me use a known working construction:
        # For any n: if n is odd, "a"*(n//2) + "b" + "a"*(n//2)
        # For even n: "a"*(n//2) + "b" + "a"*(n//2 - 1) -- length n, but 'a' appears n-1 times (odd if n-1 odd i.e. n even) yes!
        # 'a' appears n-1 times (odd since n is even means n-1 is odd) ✓
        # 'b' appears 1 time (odd) ✓
        # 'aa' appears n-2 times... n-2 when n is even: n-2 is even. BAD.
        #
        # Use all distinct characters when n <= 26, else use the odd-length trick with padding
        # Actually simplest correct solution: just use 'a'*n when n==1, 
        # and for general n use n distinct chars if n<=26, 
        # otherwise build palindrome of odd length
        half = n // 2
        # even: a^(half) b a^(half-1) has length n, 'a' count = n-1 (odd), 'b' count=1 (odd)
        # 'aa' count = n-2 (even when n even) -- PROBLEM
        # Use completely different chars to avoid repeated substrings:
        # Just output n//2 pairs... 
        # KNOWN SOLUTION: for odd n use a^k b a^k, for even n use a^k b a^k c (k=(n-2)//2... no)
        # For even n: a^(n//2) + b + a^(n//2 - 1) -- 'aa..a'(len 2) appears n-2 times
        # Let's just use all distinct if possible, else odd palindrome
        if n <= 26:
            s = ''.join(chr(ord('a') + i) for i in range(n))
        else:
            # n > 26 and even: make it odd by using odd palindrome of length n
            # We'll use: 'a'*(n//2) + 'b' + 'a'*(n//2 - 1) but fix the even count issue
            # by inserting a unique char in middle
            # Actually for large n, use: a single char repeated odd times won't work for n>1
            # Use: 'ab' * (n//4) ... 
            # SIMPLEST: reduce to odd case
            # if n even, output same as n-1 (odd) + one extra unique char
            odd_n = n - 1
            half2 = odd_n // 2
            s = 'a' * half2 + 'b' + 'a' * half2 + 'c'
    print(s)

pipelines.final_code:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    if n % 2 == 1:
        # odd length: use n//2 'a's + 'b' + n//2 'a's
        half = n // 2
        s = 'a' * half + 'b' + 'a' * half
    else:
        # even length: use (n//2 - 1) 'a's + 'b' + 'a' + (n//2 - 1) 'a's + ... 
        # Actually, let's think differently:
        # For even n: use 'ab' repeated? No that won't work.
        # Simple approach: for even n, use (n-1) unique chars approach or
        # n//2 'a's + 'b' + (n//2 - 1) 'a's won't be symmetric
        # 
        # Better: if n is even, we can do (n//2) 'a' + (n//2) 'b'
        # "aaa...abbb...b" - check: 'a' occurs n//2 times (odd if n//2 is odd)
        # This doesn't always work.
        #
        # Reliable approach: 
        # If n is even: output 'a'*(n//2 - 1) + 'b' + 'a' + 'b' + 'a'*(n//2 - 1)
        # Hmm, let me use a known working construction:
        # For any n: if n is odd, "a"*(n//2) + "b" + "a"*(n//2)
        # For even n: "a"*(n//2) + "b" + "a"*(n//2 - 1) -- length n, but 'a' appears n-1 times (odd if n-1 odd i.e. n even) yes!
        # 'a' appears n-1 times (odd since n is even means n-1 is odd) ✓
        # 'b' appears 1 time (odd) ✓
        # 'aa' appears n-2 times... n-2 when n is even: n-2 is even. BAD.
        #
        # Use all distinct characters when n <= 26, else use the odd-length trick with padding
        # Actually simplest correct solution: just use 'a'*n when n==1, 
        # and for general n use n distinct chars if n<=26, 
        # otherwise build palindrome of odd length
        half = n // 2
        # even: a^(half) b a^(half-1) has length n, 'a' count = n-1 (odd), 'b' count=1 (odd)
        # 'aa' count = n-2 (even when n even) -- PROBLEM
        # Use completely different chars to avoid repeated substrings:
        # Just output n//2 pairs... 
        # KNOWN SOLUTION: for odd n use a^k b a^k, for even n use a^k b a^k c (k=(n-2)//2... no)
        # For even n: a^(n//2) + b + a^(n//2 - 1) -- 'aa..a'(len 2) appears n-2 times
        # Let's just use all distinct if possible, else odd palindrome
        if n <= 26:
            s = ''.join(chr(ord('a') + i) for i in range(n))
        else:
            # n > 26 and even: make it odd by using odd palindrome of length n
            # We'll use: 'a'*(n//2) + 'b' + 'a'*(n//2 - 1) but fix the even count issue
            # by inserting a unique char in middle
            # Actually for large n, use: a single char repeated odd times won't work for n>1
            # Use: 'ab' * (n//4) ... 
            # SIMPLEST: reduce to odd case
            # if n even, output same as n-1 (odd) + one extra unique char
            odd_n = n - 1
            half2 = odd_n // 2
            s = 'a' * half2 + 'b' + 'a' * half2 + 'c'
    print(s)
